title: Why the Lair Stands Here
description: The resident's reason for choosing this spot — original table authored for the lair composite (§10.6 review: 'Why Here' used to read a dungeon-room description as a motive).
entries: The only {pick:fresh water|spring|pool} for a day's travel rises here, and everything thirsty must come to it.
A {pick:herd trail|migration route|drover's road} passes below — the hunting comes to the den.
The {pick:old ruin|fallen watchtower|collapsed mine} gave it walls no burrow could match.
One narrow {pick:defile|ledge|crawlway} is the only way in, and nothing larger than the resident can use it.
It was {pick:driven from richer country|wounded and ran until it could run no farther|the last survivor of its brood} — this is where the running stopped.
Something {pick:warm|foul|strange} seeps from the earth here, and the resident {pick:needs it|craves it|guards it}.
The den sits {pick:downwind of the village|above the fog line|below the frost line} — it can hunt without ever being scented.
A previous occupant {pick:died here|was driven out|left in a hurry}, and its hoard was too heavy to move.
The ground remembers {pick:an old battle|a burial field|a plague pit}; easy pickings for a thing that eats the dead.
It is {pick:waiting for something|guarding something|hiding from something}, and this is the appointed place.
The {pick:caves|hollows} run deeper than any map admits — endless bolt-holes if hunters ever come.
Sheer {pick:cliffs|bogland|scree} on three sides: one approach to watch, and it watches.
The locals {pick:leave tribute|drive out their old livestock|stopped fighting back years ago} — why hunt harder country?
An old {pick:pact|curse|binding} chains it to this ground, whether it remembers agreeing or not.
